tex:
(a) In a café at a train station, a cup of coffee costs \$3.25 and a glass of cola costs \$2.15.
\newline
Gary buys 2 cups of coffee and 4 glasses of cola.
\newline
Work out how much change he receives from a \$20 note.
\newline

\$ 

(b) Roy spends \$37.80 in the café on food and drink in the ratio food : drink = 7 : 2.
\newline
Work out how much he spends on food.
\newline

\$ 

(c) The price of a \$48 train ticket is increased by 12\%.
\newline
Find the new price of the ticket.
\newline

\$ 

(d) Here is part of the timetable for trains from Washby to Dunstley.
\newline
All trains take the same time to travel from Washby to Dunstley.
\newline

Washby

09 18

11 05

Dunstley

10 03

Complete the timetable.
\newline

(e) On one day, Washby station sells 28 senior tickets, 192 adult tickets and some child tickets.
\newline

Senior tickets

42°

Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline

content.content:
In a café at a train station, a cup of coffee costs \$3.25 and a glass of cola costs \$2.15.
\newline
Gary buys 2 cups of coffee and 4 glasses of cola.
\newline
Work out how much change he receives from a \$20 note.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[]

\newline

\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[]

\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[]

Here is part of the timetable for trains from Washby to Dunstley.
\newline

\newline

Washby

09 18

11 05

Dunstley

10 03

.........................

Complete the timetable.
\newline

On one day, Washby station sells 28 senior tickets, 192 adult tickets and some child tickets.
\newline

Senior tickets

42°

Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item In a café at a train station, a cup of coffee costs \$3.25 and a glass of cola costs \$2.15.
Gary buys 2 cups of coffee and 4 glasses of cola.
Work out how much change he receives from a \$20 note.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}

\item Roy spends \$37.80 in the café on food and drink in the ratio food : drink = 7 : 2.
Work out how much he spends on food.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item The price of a \$48 train ticket is increased by 12\%.
Find the new price of the ticket.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item Here is part of the timetable for trains from Washby to Dunstley.
All trains take the same time to travel from Washby to Dunstley.

Washby

09 18

11 05

Dunstley

10 03

.........................

Complete the timetable.

\Marks{2}

\item On one day, Washby station sells 28 senior tickets, 192 adult tickets and some child tickets.

Senior tickets

42°

Complete the pie chart to show this information.

\Marks{3}
\end{parts}

qtype: number properties, prime factorization, LCM, estimation
tex:
(a) From this list of numbers, write down
8

17

26

35

49

51

72

(i) (b) a multiple of 24,

(c) a square number,

(d) a cube number,

(e) a prime number.
\newline

(f) Write 420 as a product of its prime factors.
\newline

(g) Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 30 and 84.
\newline

(h) By writing each number correct to 1 significant figure, show that an estimate for this
calculation is 40.
\newline
9.875 + 18.305
+ 27.837
3.418

content.content:

8

17

26

35

49

51

72
a multiple of 24,

a prime number.

+ 27.837

tex:
(a) Simone completes one lap of a 400 metre running track in 79 seconds.
\newline
Work out how long it will take her to run 6 km at the same rate.
\newline
Give your answer in minutes and seconds.
\newline

 minutes  seconds

(b) The probability that she does not win a race is 0.94.
\newline
Find the probability that she wins a race.
\newline

(c) Each day she records the number of laps she runs.
\newline
Here is her record for one week.
\newline
15

42

28

16

24

15

32

(i) (d) Write down the mode.
\newline

(e) Find the median.
\newline

(f) Find the range.
\newline

(g) Wilfred records his times, in seconds, for each of 5 laps.
\newline
59

74

69

63

65

After running a 6th lap his mean time is 67 seconds.
\newline
Find his time for the 6th lap.
\newline

 seconds

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item Simone completes one lap of a 400 metre running track in 79 seconds.
Work out how long it will take her to run 6 km at the same rate.
Give your answer in minutes and seconds.

\AnswerLine[] minutes \AnswerLine[] seconds
\Marks{4}

\item The probability that she does not win a race is 0.94.
Find the probability that she wins a race.

\AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item Each day she records the number of laps she runs.
Here is her record for one week.
15

42

28

16

24

15

32

\begin{subparts}
\item Write down the mode.

\AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item Find the median.

\AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item Find the range.

\AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}
\end{subparts}

\item Wilfred records his times, in seconds, for each of 5 laps.
59

74

69

63

65

After running a 6th lap his mean time is 67 seconds.
Find his time for the 6th lap.

\AnswerLine[] seconds
\Marks{3}
\end{parts}

the population of China = k $\times$ the population of Eritrea.
